front: What is the core concept of Abstraction in Object-Oriented Programming?
back: Abstraction is the process of hiding implementation details and showing only essential features or functionality to the user. It reduces complexity by allowing the programmer to focus on interactions at a high level rather than low-level details.

front: How is abstraction primarily achieved in Java?
back: Abstraction in Java is primarily achieved through abstract classes (using the `abstract` keyword) and interfaces. These mechanisms allow you to define a blueprint for other classes without providing a complete implementation.

front: What is an abstract class in Java?
back: An abstract class is a class declared with the `abstract` keyword. It cannot be instantiated and may contain a mix of abstract methods (without a body) and concrete methods (with a body). It serves as a template for subclasses to extend and implement.

An abstract method is a method declared without an implementation (i.e., without a body). It is declared using the `abstract` keyword and ends with a semicolon. Any class that extends an abstract class containing abstract methods must provide implementations for them, unless it is also abstract.

front: What is the key difference between an abstract class and an interface in the context of abstraction?
back: An abstract class can have both abstract and concrete methods, constructors, and state (instance variables). An interface (prior to Java 8) could only declare abstract methods. From Java 8 onwards, interfaces can have default and static methods, but they still cannot have instance fields or constructors like an abstract class can. A class can implement multiple interfaces but extend only one abstract class.
